You are a Solana smart contract security analyzer. Analyze the following Rust code for vulnerabilities.

Code:
fn calculate_deposit_amount(
    pre_token_supply: u64,
    pre_pool_stake: u64,
    user_stake_to_deposit: u64,
) -> Option<u64> {
    if pre_pool_stake == 0 || pre_token_supply == 0 {
        Some(user_stake_to_deposit)
    } else {
        u64::try_from(
            (user_stake_to_deposit as u128)
                .checked_mul(pre_token_supply as u128)?
                .checked_div(pre_pool_stake as u128)?,
        )
        .ok()
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V8 - Integer Overflow - MITIGATED
u128 promotion prevents intermediate overflow. checked_mul and checked_div return None on overflow/zero-division. u64::try_from validates the result fits in u64. Returns Option for caller to handle failure.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction

fn calculate_deposit_amount(
    pre_token_supply: u64,
    pre_pool_stake: u64,
    user_stake_to_deposit: u64,
) -> u64 {
    if pre_pool_stake == 0 || pre_token_supply == 0 {
        user_stake_to_deposit
    } else {
        (user_stake_to_deposit * pre_token_supply) / pre_pool_stake
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V8 - Integer Overflow
CRITICAL
Multiplication of two u64 values can overflow u64 range (max ~1.8e19). Without u128 promotion, large stake deposits with large token supplies silently wrap, minting incorrect token amounts. Division by zero is possible if pre_pool_stake is 0 despite the guard.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced u128 promotion with u64 arithmetic, replaced checked_mul/checked_div with unchecked * and /, removed u64::try_from validation, changed return from Option<u64> to u64

fn calculate_withdraw_amount(
    pre_token_supply: u64,
    pre_pool_stake: u64,
    user_tokens_to_burn: u64,
) -> Option<u64> {
    let numerator = (user_tokens_to_burn as u128).checked_mul(pre_pool_stake as u128)?;
    let denominator = pre_token_supply as u128;
    if numerator < denominator || denominator == 0 {
        Some(0)
    } else {
        u64::try_from(numerator.checked_div(denominator)?).ok()
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V8 - Integer Overflow - MITIGATED
u128 promotion, checked_mul, denominator zero check, dust withdrawal protection (returns 0 if numerator < denominator), and u64::try_from validation.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction

fn process_deposit_stake(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let (_, pool_stake_state) = get_stake_state(pool_stake_info)?;
    let pre_pool_stake = pool_stake_state
        .delegation
        .stake
        .saturating_sub(minimum_pool_balance);
    let pre_pool_excess_lamports = pool_stake_info
        .lamports()
        .checked_sub(pool_stake_state.delegation.stake)
        .and_then(|amount| amount.checked_sub(pool_rent_exempt_reserve))
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::ArithmeticOverflow)?;

    // After merge
    let (_, pool_stake_state) = get_stake_state(pool_stake_info)?;
    let post_pool_stake = pool_stake_state
        .delegation
        .stake
        .saturating_sub(minimum_pool_balance);

    let stake_added = post_pool_stake
        .checked_sub(pre_pool_stake)
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::ArithmeticOverflow)?;

    let user_excess_lamports = post_pool_lamports
        .checked_sub(pool_stake_state.delegation.stake)
        .and_then(|amount| amount.checked_sub(pool_rent_exempt_reserve))
        .and_then(|amount| amount.checked_sub(pre_pool_excess_lamports))
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::ArithmeticOverflow)?;

    let new_pool_tokens = calculate_deposit_amount(token_supply, pre_pool_stake, stake_added)
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::UnexpectedMathError)?;

    if new_pool_tokens == 0 {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::DepositTooSmall.into());
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V8 - Integer Overflow - MITIGATED
Multiple chained checked_sub operations with ArithmeticOverflow error propagation. calculate_deposit_amount returns Option handled with ok_or. Zero token check prevents dust deposits.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept checked_sub chains (L988-996, L1032-1042), zero deposit check (L1059-1061), calculate_deposit_amount with ok_or (L1056-1057)

pub fn process_transfer(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
    transfer_instruction: TransferInstruction,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let source_amount = u64::from(source_account.base.amount);
    if source_amount < amount {
        return Err(TokenError::InsufficientFunds.into());
    }

    source_account.base.amount = source_amount
        .checked_sub(amount)
        .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?
        .into();
    let credited_amount = amount
        .checked_sub(calculated_fee)
        .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?;
    destination_account.base.amount = u64::from(destination_account.base.amount)
        .checked_add(credited_amount)
        .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?
        .into();

    if calculated_fee > 0 {
        if let Ok(extension) = destination_account.get_extension_mut::<TransferFeeAmount>() {
            let new_withheld_amount = u64::from(extension.withheld_amount)
                .checked_add(calculated_fee)
                .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?;
            extension.withheld_amount = new_withheld_amount.into();
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V8 - Integer Overflow - MITIGATED
All balance mutations use checked arithmetic: checked_sub for deductions (source amount and fee), checked_add for credits (destination and withheld fee). InsufficientFunds pre-check prevents unnecessary computation.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept checked_sub for source (L535-538), checked_sub for fee (L539-541), checked_add for destination (L542-545), checked_add for withheld_amount (L548-550)

pub fn process_transfer(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let source_amount = u64::from(source_account.base.amount);

    source_account.base.amount = (source_amount - amount).into();
    let credited_amount = amount - calculated_fee;
    destination_account.base.amount =
        (u64::from(destination_account.base.amount) + credited_amount).into();

    if calculated_fee > 0 {
        if let Ok(extension) = destination_account.get_extension_mut::<TransferFeeAmount>() {
            extension.withheld_amount =
                (u64::from(extension.withheld_amount) + calculated_fee).into();
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V8 - Integer Overflow
CRITICAL
Unchecked subtraction on source allows underflow if amount > balance, wrapping to near-max u64. Unchecked addition on destination can overflow. Without InsufficientFunds check, transfers of more tokens than available succeed.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - replaced checked_sub (L535-538) with unchecked -, replaced checked_add (L542-545) with unchecked +, removed InsufficientFunds check (L362-364)

pub fn process_mint_to(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
    instruction_variant: InstructionVariant,
) -> ProgramResult {
    check_program_account(mint_info.owner)?;
    check_program_account(destination_account_info.owner)?;

    if destination_account.base.is_frozen() {
        return Err(TokenError::AccountFrozen.into());
    }
    if destination_account.base.is_native() {
        return Err(TokenError::NativeNotSupported.into());
    }
    if mint_info.key != &destination_account.base.mint {
        return Err(TokenError::MintMismatch.into());
    }

    match &mint.base.mint_authority {
        PodCOption { option: PodCOption::<Pubkey>::SOME, value: mint_authority } =>
            Self::validate_owner(program_id, mint_authority, owner_info,
                owner_info_data_len, account_info_iter.as_slice())?,
        _ => return Err(TokenError::FixedSupply.into()),
    }

    destination_account.base.amount = u64::from(destination_account.base.amount)
        .checked_add(amount)
        .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?
        .into();
    mint.base.supply = u64::from(mint.base.supply)
        .checked_add(amount)
        .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?
        .into();
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V8 - Integer Overflow - MITIGATED
checked_add on both destination amount and mint supply prevents overflow. FixedSupply check prevents minting when authority is revoked. Frozen, native, and mint mismatch validations.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept checked_add for amount (L1102-1105) and supply (L1107-1110), validate_owner (L1092-1098), frozen/native/mint checks

fn process_deposit_stake(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    SinglePool::from_account_info(pool_info, program_id)?;
    check_pool_stake_address(program_id, pool_info.key, pool_stake_info.key)?;

    if pool_stake_info.key == user_stake_info.key {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::InvalidPoolStakeAccountUsage.into());
    }
    let onramp_account_address = crate::find_pool_onramp_address(program_id, pool_info.key);
    if onramp_account_address == *user_stake_info.key {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::InvalidPoolStakeAccountUsage.into());
    }

    let new_pool_tokens = calculate_deposit_amount(token_supply, pre_pool_stake, stake_added)
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::UnexpectedMathError)?;
    if new_pool_tokens == 0 {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::DepositTooSmall.into());
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
Self-deposit prevention blocks using pool's own stake or onramp as deposit source. Zero token check prevents dust deposits that waste compute. Pool account validation ensures correct accounts.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept self-deposit prevention (L973-980), zero token check (L1059-1061), pool account validation

fn process_withdraw_stake(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    user_stake_authority: &Pubkey,
    token_amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    SinglePool::from_account_info(pool_info, program_id)?;
    check_pool_stake_address(program_id, pool_info.key, pool_stake_info.key)?;

    if pool_stake_info.key == user_stake_info.key {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::InvalidPoolStakeAccountUsage.into());
    }

    let withdraw_stake = calculate_withdraw_amount(token_supply, pre_pool_stake, token_amount)
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::UnexpectedMathError)?;

    if withdraw_stake == 0 {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::WithdrawalTooSmall.into());
    }
    if withdraw_stake > pre_pool_stake || withdraw_stake == pool_stake_info.lamports() {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::WithdrawalTooLarge.into());
    }

    // Burn FIRST, then split
    Self::token_burn(pool_info.key, token_program_info.clone(),
        user_token_account_info.clone(), pool_mint_info.clone(),
        pool_mint_authority_info.clone(), mint_authority_bump_seed, token_amount)?;

    Self::stake_split(pool_info.key, pool_stake_info.clone(),
        pool_stake_authority_info.clone(), stake_authority_bump_seed,
        withdraw_stake, user_stake_info.clone())?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
WithdrawalTooSmall prevents zero-stake splits (which fail at runtime). WithdrawalTooLarge prevents draining the entire pool stake (which would kill the pool). Self-stake prevention blocks using pool accounts as withdrawal target.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept withdrawal size validation (L1160-1167), self-stake prevention (L1135-1142), burn-then-split ordering (L1170-1188)

pub fn process_close_account(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    check_program_account(source_account_info.owner)?;

    if source_account_info.key == destination_account_info.key {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData);
    }

    let source_account = PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodAccount>::unpack(&mut source_account_data)?;

    if !source_account.base.is_native() && u64::from(source_account.base.amount) != 0 {
        return Err(TokenError::NonNativeHasBalance.into());
    }

    let authority = source_account
        .base
        .close_authority
        .unwrap_or(source_account.base.owner);
    Self::validate_owner(
        program_id, &authority, authority_info,
        authority_info_data_len, account_info_iter.as_slice(),
    )?;

    if let Ok(cpi_guard) = source_account.get_extension::<CpiGuard>() {
        if cpi_guard.lock_cpi.into() && in_cpi() {
            return Err(TokenError::CpiGuardCloseAccountBlocked.into());
        }
    }

    let destination_starting_lamports = destination_account_info.lamports();
    **destination_account_info.lamports.borrow_mut() = destination_starting_lamports
        .checked_add(source_account_info.lamports())
        .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?;
    **source_account_info.lamports.borrow_mut() = 0;

    source_account.base.amount = 0.into();
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V10 - Denial of Service - MITIGATED
Self-close prevention, non-zero balance check (prevents accidental token loss), CPI guard prevents unauthorized closes via CPI, and checked_add for lamport transfer prevents overflow.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept self-close prevention (L1298-1300), non-zero balance check (L1309-1311), validate_owner, CPI guard check (L1321-1325), checked_add for lamports

Code:
fn process_deposit_stake(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    // Validate user stake authorized to pool
    let (user_stake_meta, user_stake_state) = get_stake_state(user_stake_info)?;
    if user_stake_meta.authorized != stake::state::Authorized::auto(pool_stake_authority_info.key) {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::WrongStakeStake.into());
    }

    // FIRST: Merge user stake into pool (takes user's stake)
    Self::stake_merge(pool_info.key, user_stake_info.clone(),
        pool_stake_authority_info.clone(), stake_authority_bump_seed,
        pool_stake_info.clone(), clock_info.clone(), stake_history_info.clone())?;

    // Sanity check: user stake account is empty after merge
    if user_stake_info.lamports() != 0 {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::UnexpectedMathError.into());
    }

    // THEN: Mint tokens to user
    Self::token_mint_to(pool_info.key, token_program_info.clone(),
        pool_mint_info.clone(), user_token_account_info.clone(),
        pool_mint_authority_info.clone(), mint_authority_bump_seed, new_pool_tokens)?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Correct CEI ordering: user's stake is merged into pool FIRST, then tokens are minted. Post-merge sanity check verifies user account is empty. Even during mint CPI callback, user's stake is already consumed.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept stake authorization check (L1002-1008), merge-then-mint CPI ordering (L1013-1021 merge, L1064-1072 mint), post-merge sanity check (L1045-1047)

fn process_deposit_stake(program_id: &Pubkey, accounts: &[AccountInfo]) -> ProgramResult {
    let stake_added = user_stake_state.delegation.stake;

    let new_pool_tokens = calculate_deposit_amount(token_supply, pre_pool_stake, stake_added)
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::UnexpectedMathError)?;

    // WRONG: Mint tokens FIRST
    Self::token_mint_to(pool_info.key, token_program_info.clone(),
        pool_mint_info.clone(), user_token_account_info.clone(),
        pool_mint_authority_info.clone(), mint_authority_bump_seed, new_pool_tokens)?;

    // THEN: Merge stake
    Self::stake_merge(pool_info.key, user_stake_info.clone(),
        pool_stake_authority_info.clone(), stake_authority_bump_seed,
        pool_stake_info.clone(), clock_info.clone(), stake_history_info.clone())?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks
CRITICAL
Tokens are minted before user's stake is merged. During mint CPI, attacker can use the newly minted tokens to withdraw from the pool. The attacker gets pool tokens without the stake merge completing.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: modified from source - reversed CPI order: token_mint_to (originally L1064) executes before stake_merge (originally L1013), removed post-merge sanity check (L1045-1047)

    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    user_stake_authority: &Pubkey,
    token_amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    // FIRST: Burn user's pool tokens (destroys withdrawal authorization)
    Self::token_burn(pool_info.key, token_program_info.clone(),
        user_token_account_info.clone(), pool_mint_info.clone(),
        pool_mint_authority_info.clone(), mint_authority_bump_seed, token_amount)?;

    // SECOND: Split stake from pool to user
    Self::stake_split(pool_info.key, pool_stake_info.clone(),
        pool_stake_authority_info.clone(), stake_authority_bump_seed,
        withdraw_stake, user_stake_info.clone())?;

    // LAST: Assign authorities to user
    Self::stake_authorize(pool_info.key, user_stake_info.clone(),
        pool_stake_authority_info.clone(), stake_authority_bump_seed,
        user_stake_authority, clock_info.clone())?;

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
Correct CEI pattern: tokens are burned FIRST (destroying withdrawal right), then stake is split, then authorities are assigned. Any reentrancy during split/authorize would fail because tokens are already burned.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept burn-then-split-then-authorize CPI ordering

Code:
pub fn process_transfer(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let source_account = PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodAccount>::unpack(&mut source_account_data)?;

    if let Ok(cpi_guard) = source_account.get_extension::<CpiGuard>() {
        if *authority_info.key == source_account.base.owner
            && cpi_guard.lock_cpi.into()
            && in_cpi()
        {
            return Err(TokenError::CpiGuardTransferBlocked.into());
        }
    }

    Self::validate_owner(program_id, &source_account.base.owner,
        authority_info, authority_info_data_len, account_info_iter.as_slice())?;

    // Transfer hook: set flags, drop borrows, invoke, unset flags
    if let Some(program_id) = maybe_transfer_hook_program_id {
        transfer_hook::set_transferring(&mut source_account)?;
        transfer_hook::set_transferring(&mut destination_account)?;
        drop(source_account_data);
        drop(destination_account_data);
        spl_transfer_hook_interface::onchain::invoke_execute(
            &program_id, source_account_info.clone(), mint_info.clone(),
            destination_account_info.clone(), authority_info.clone(),
            account_info_iter.as_slice(), amount,
        )?;
        transfer_hook::unset_transferring(source_account_info)?;
        transfer_hook::unset_transferring(destination_account_info)?;
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V5 - Reentrancy Attacks - MITIGATED
CPI guard blocks owner-authorized transfers when called via CPI. Transfer hook uses set_transferring/unset_transferring flags to mark accounts as in-transit, preventing reentrancy during hook execution. Borrows are dropped before CPI to avoid double-borrow.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept CPI guard check (L445-456), transfer hook flag mechanism (L575-593)

fn _process_initialize_mint(
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    decimals: u8,
    mint_authority: &Pubkey,
    freeze_authority: PodCOption<Pubkey>,
    rent_sysvar_account: bool,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    check_program_account(mint_info.owner)?;

    if !rent.is_exempt(mint_info.lamports(), mint_data_len) {
        return Err(TokenError::NotRentExempt.into());
    }

    let mut mint = PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodMint>::unpack_uninitialized(&mut mint_data)?;
    let extension_types = mint.get_extension_types()?;
    if ExtensionType::try_calculate_account_len::<Mint>(&extension_types)? != mint_data_len {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData);
    }
    ExtensionType::check_for_invalid_mint_extension_combinations(&extension_types)?;

    if let Ok(default_account_state) = mint.get_extension_mut::<DefaultAccountState>() {
        let state = AccountState::try_from(default_account_state.state)
            .or(Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData))?;
        if state == AccountState::Frozen && freeze_authority.is_none() {
            return Err(TokenError::MintCannotFreeze.into());
        }
    }

    mint.base.mint_authority = PodCOption::some(*mint_authority);
    mint.base.decimals = decimals;
    mint.base.is_initialized = PodBool::from_bool(true);
    mint.base.freeze_authority = freeze_authority;
    mint.init_account_type()?;
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V4 - Input Validation - MITIGATED
Program ownership check, rent exemption, unpack_uninitialized prevents re-initialization, extension type validation ensures correct account size and valid combinations, frozen-without-freeze-authority prevention.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction

fn _process_initialize_account(
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    owner: Option<&Pubkey>,
    rent_sysvar_account: bool,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let new_account_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;
    let mint_info = next_account_info(account_info_iter)?;

    check_program_account(new_account_info.owner)?;
    check_program_account(mint_info.owner)?;

    if !rent.is_exempt(new_account_info.lamports(), new_account_info_data_len) {
        return Err(TokenError::NotRentExempt.into());
    }

    let mint_data = mint_info.data.borrow();
    let mint = PodStateWithExtensions::<PodMint>::unpack(&mint_data)
        .map_err(|_| Into::<ProgramError>::into(TokenError::InvalidMint))?;

    let required_extensions =
        Self::get_required_account_extensions_from_unpacked_mint(mint_info.owner, &mint)?;
    if ExtensionType::try_calculate_account_len::<Account>(&required_extensions)?
        > new_account_info_data_len
    {
        return Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData);
    }

    let starting_state =
        if let Ok(default_account_state) = mint.get_extension::<DefaultAccountState>() {
            AccountState::try_from(default_account_state.state)
                .or(Err(ProgramError::InvalidAccountData))?
        } else {
            AccountState::Initialized
        };

    account.base.mint = *mint_info.key;
    account.base.owner = *owner;
    account.base.state = starting_state.into();

    if mint_info.key == &native_mint::id() {
        account.base.amount = new_account_info.lamports()
            .checked_sub(rent_exempt_reserve)
            .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?
            .into();
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V4 - Input Validation - MITIGATED
Dual program ownership check (account and mint), rent exemption, mint validity verification, extension requirements derived from mint, default account state handling, and checked arithmetic for native mint.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept dual check_program_account (L177-178), rent check (L197-199), mint validation (L203-204), extension requirements (L212-218), default state handling (L223-229), native mint checked_sub (L240-244)

fn check_vote_account(vote_account_info: &AccountInfo) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
    check_account_owner(vote_account_info, &vote_program::id())?;

    let vote_account_data = &vote_account_info.try_borrow_data()?;
    let state_variant = vote_account_data
        .get(..VOTE_STATE_DISCRIMINATOR_END)
        .and_then(|s| s.try_into().ok())
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::UnparseableVoteAccount)?;

    match u32::from_le_bytes(state_variant) {
        1 | 2 | 3 => Ok(()),
        0 => Err(SinglePoolError::LegacyVoteAccount.into()),
        _ => Err(SinglePoolError::UnparseableVoteAccount.into()),
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V4 - Input Validation - MITIGATED
Ownership check ensures account is owned by vote program. Discriminator parsing validates the vote state version. Legacy vote accounts (version 0) are explicitly rejected. Unparseable accounts return error.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

fn token_mint_to<'a>(
    pool_account_key: &Pubkey,
    token_program: AccountInfo<'a>,
    mint: AccountInfo<'a>,
    destination: AccountInfo<'a>,
    authority: AccountInfo<'a>,
    bump_seed: u8,
    amount: u64,
) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
    let authority_seeds = &[POOL_MINT_AUTHORITY_PREFIX, pool_account_key.as_ref(), &[bump_seed]];
    let signers = &[&authority_seeds[..]];

    let ix = spl_token::instruction::mint_to(
        token_program.key, mint.key, destination.key, authority.key, &[], amount,
    )?;

    invoke_signed(&ix, &[mint, destination, authority], signers)
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

fn stake_split<'a>(
    pool_account_key: &Pubkey,
    stake_account: AccountInfo<'a>,
    authority: AccountInfo<'a>,
    bump_seed: u8,
    amount: u64,
    split_stake: AccountInfo<'a>,
) -> Result<(), ProgramError> {
    let authority_seeds = &[POOL_STAKE_AUTHORITY_PREFIX, pool_account_key.as_ref(), &[bump_seed]];
    let signers = &[&authority_seeds[..]];

    let split_instruction =
        stake::instruction::split(stake_account.key, authority.key, amount, split_stake.key);

    invoke_signed(
        split_instruction.last().unwrap(),
        &[stake_account, split_stake, authority],
        signers,
    )
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

VULNERABLE
V6 - Unchecked External Calls
MEDIUM
The .last().unwrap() on the split instruction vector can panic if the vector is empty. While stake::instruction::split is unlikely to return an empty vector in practice, using unwrap instead of proper error handling is a vulnerability pattern. Should use .last().ok_or(ProgramError::...)? instead.<|eot_id|>
label: VULNERABLE

source_info.extraction_method: direct extraction - .last().unwrap() on instruction vector (L409) is a code smell that panics if vector is empty

fn check_pool_pda(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    base_address: &Pubkey,
    check_address: &Pubkey,
    pda_lookup_fn: &dyn Fn(&Pubkey, &Pubkey) -> (Pubkey, u8),
    pda_name: &str,
    pool_error: SinglePoolError,
) -> Result<u8, ProgramError> {
    let (derived_address, bump_seed) = pda_lookup_fn(program_id, base_address);
    if *check_address != derived_address {
        msg!("Incorrect {} address for base {}: expected {}, received {}",
            pda_name, base_address, derived_address, check_address);
        Err(pool_error.into())
    } else {
        Ok(bump_seed)
    }
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

pub fn process_transfer(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    check_program_account(source_account_info.owner)?;
    check_program_account(destination_account_info.owner)?;

    let source_account = PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodAccount>::unpack(&mut source_account_data)?;
    if source_account.base.is_frozen() {
        return Err(TokenError::AccountFrozen.into());
    }

    if let Ok(cpi_guard) = source_account.get_extension::<CpiGuard>() {
        if *authority_info.key == source_account.base.owner
            && cpi_guard.lock_cpi.into() && in_cpi()
        {
            return Err(TokenError::CpiGuardTransferBlocked.into());
        }
    }

    match (source_account.base.delegate, maybe_permanent_delegate) {
        (_, Some(ref delegate)) if authority_info.key == delegate =>
            Self::validate_owner(program_id, delegate, authority_info,
                authority_info_data_len, account_info_iter.as_slice())?,
        (PodCOption { option: PodCOption::<Pubkey>::SOME, value: delegate }, _)
            if authority_info.key == &delegate => {
            Self::validate_owner(program_id, &delegate, authority_info,
                authority_info_data_len, account_info_iter.as_slice())?;
            if u64::from(source_account.base.delegated_amount) < amount {
                return Err(TokenError::InsufficientFunds.into());
            }
            source_account.base.delegated_amount = u64::from(source_account.base.delegated_amount)
                .checked_sub(amount).ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?.into();
        }
        _ => Self::validate_owner(program_id, &source_account.base.owner,
            authority_info, authority_info_data_len, account_info_iter.as_slice())?,
    }
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V1 - Access Control - MITIGATED
Three-way authority validation: permanent delegate, account delegate, and owner — each with validate_owner. CPI guard adds extra protection against unauthorized CPI-based transfers. Frozen account check prevents transfers from frozen accounts.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept validate_owner for all three authority paths (permanent delegate L458-464, delegate L472-491, owner L494-500), CPI guard (L445-456), frozen check (L358-360)

pub fn process_transfer(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    amount: u64,
) -> ProgramResult {
    let source_account = PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodAccount>::unpack(&mut source_account_data)?;

    let source_amount = u64::from(source_account.base.amount);
    source_account.base.amount = source_amount
        .checked_sub(amount)
        .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?
        .into();

    let mut destination_account_data = destination_account_info.data.borrow_mut();
    let destination_account =
        PodStateWithExtensionsMut::<PodAccount>::unpack(&mut destination_account_data)?;
    destination_account.base.amount = u64::from(destination_account.base.amount)
        .checked_add(amount)
        .ok_or(TokenError::Overflow)?
        .into();
    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

fn process_update_pool_token_metadata(
    program_id: &Pubkey,
    accounts: &[AccountInfo],
    name: String,
    symbol: String,
    uri: String,
) -> ProgramResult {
    check_vote_account(vote_account_info)?;
    check_pool_address(program_id, vote_account_info.key, pool_info.key)?;

    let pool = SinglePool::from_account_info(pool_info, program_id)?;
    if pool.vote_account_address != *vote_account_info.key {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::InvalidPoolAccount.into());
    }

    let vote_account_data = &vote_account_info.try_borrow_data()?;
    let vote_account_withdrawer = vote_account_data
        .get(VOTE_STATE_AUTHORIZED_WITHDRAWER_START..VOTE_STATE_AUTHORIZED_WITHDRAWER_END)
        .and_then(|x| Pubkey::try_from(x).ok())
        .ok_or(SinglePoolError::UnparseableVoteAccount)?;

    if *authorized_withdrawer_info.key != vote_account_withdrawer {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::InvalidMetadataSigner.into());
    }
    if !authorized_withdrawer_info.is_signer {
        return Err(SinglePoolError::SignatureMissing.into());
    }

    Ok(())
}<|eot_id|><|start_header_id|>assistant<|end_header_id|>

SAFE
V1 - Access Control - MITIGATED
Vote account ownership verified. Pool linked to correct vote account. Authorized withdrawer extracted from on-chain vote state data and matched against provided account. Signature required. Only the validator operator can update metadata.<|eot_id|>
label: SAFE

source_info.extraction_method: extracted and simplified - kept vote account check (L1303-1304), pool-vote link (L1307-1309), withdrawer key extraction and match (L1324-1333), signer check (L1335-1338)
